title: Ignore file (`.dockerignore`) does not parse leading slash correctly
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

The old `.dockerignore` I used with docker-compose.yml doesn't work.

```ignore
*

!/packages/server/assets/
!/packages/server/scripts/
!/packages/server/public/
!/packages/server/src/
!/packages/server/package.json
!/packages/server/yarn.lock
!/packages/server/tsconfig.json
!/packages/server/requirements.txt
```

But it works after some correction.

```ignore
*

!packages/server/assets/
!packages/server/scripts/
!packages/server/public/
!packages/server/src/
!packages/server/package.json
!packages/server/yarn.lock
!packages/server/tsconfig.json
!packages/server/requirements.txt
```

Now, I am curious on how podman parse ignore files.

Also, normally, I would choose to include files, rather the ignore many things.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Clone `git clone -b docker https://github.com/patarapolw/zhres.git`

2. `cd zhres && podman-compose build`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
STEP 9: COPY packages/server/package.json packages/server/yarn.lock /server/
Error: error building at STEP "COPY packages/server/package.json packages/server/yarn.lock /server/": no items matching glob "/home/polv/shared/linux/projects/zhres/packages/server/package.json" copied (1 filtered out): no such file or directory
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

No errors.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 3.2.2
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
host:
  arch: amd64

  cpus: 8
  distribution:

  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: polv-arch

    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65537
  kernel: 5.13.4-arch2-1

  ociRuntime:
    name: crun

    version: |-
      crun version 0.20.1
      commit: 38271d1c8d9641a2cdc70acfa3dcb6996d124b3d
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    exists: true
    path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true

  slirp4netns:

      libseccomp: 2.5.1
  swapFree: 8537219072

  uptime: 17h 36m 48.59s (Approximately 0.71 days)
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
➜  ~ pacman -Qs podman
local/podman 3.2.2-2
    Tool and library for running OCI-based containers in pods
local/podman-compose 0.1.5-2
    A script to run docker-compose.yml using podman
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

comments.body:
Fixed in 36e17cbb9bfa6cc30a23f81097902e18e1af0ae8

So this is fixed in the main branch right now.
We plan on shipping podman 3.3 rc1 this week, which will have the fix.
